content:
I am a PhD student in the Department of Engineering at the University of Cambridge. My current research interest includes high performance computing and reduced order methods. In particular, I’m interested in fast, high-fidelity large scale simulation of acoustics scattering problem. My PhD is under the supervision of Prof. Garth Wells.
Prior to my PhD, I’ve worked on topology optimization, computer graphics, computational fluid dynamics and vibration analysis. I’ve also spend a short stint as a trainee data scientist at Petronas and TheCADS.
summary: I am a PhD student in the Department of Engineering at the University of Cambridge. My current research interest includes high performance computing and reduced order methods. In particular, I’m interested in fast, high-fidelity large scale simulation of acoustics scattering problem.
title: Adeeb Arif Kor
Experimental Validation of Phase synchronisation effects in optimising impact-synchronous time averaging
